step.title: Gather Session Metadata
step.goal: Capture all session metadata from the thoughts CLI before any user interaction. Every downstream step depends on these values, so they must be collected first and stored in a session variable accessible to all subsequent steps.

substep.title: Run thoughts metadata
substep.note:
The script outputs a YAML document with these keys (at minimum, on a thoughts-mapped repo): - date (full ISO 8601 with timezone) - owner (the username for `owner:` and `researcher:` frontmatter) - git_commit, branch, repository - team - shared_folder (absolute path; bundle directories will be created here) - backlog_folder (NOT used by ticket-orient per spec §4.2)
On a NON-thoughts-mapped repo the output is a reduced default schema missing shared_folder and team. If that case is detected, surface a Critical error and halt — ticket-writing requires a thoughts context.

substep.title: Parse and Validate

check.action: Halt the workflow. Display: "ticket-writing requires a thoughts-mapped repository. Run `thoughts init` or navigate to a repo where shared_folder resolves, then retry."

substep.title: Display Metadata Summary

section.title: Why metadata first
section.description: Every artifact frontmatter field that ticket-orient and ticket-grill will write — `owner`, `researcher`, `date`, `last_updated`, repo context — comes from this one `thoughts metadata` call. Calling it once and caching the result avoids repeated subprocess invocations and ensures consistency across the bundle (same timestamp on all three files when scaffold-bundle.py is invoked in step 6).

rules.rule: Step 1 runs silently. No Question tool. No user-facing prompts beyond the closing summary.
The first user touchpoint is step 5 (scope checkpoint). All prior steps are autonomous orient activity.
thoughts CLI not on PATH -> halt with: "thoughts CLI not found; install and retry"
shared_folder missing -> halt as in substep 1b
Malformed YAML output -> halt and surface the raw stdout for debugging
